subtitle: Default to Postgres, until you hit one of these four walls. Each one has a number.
cover_note: A field-tested rule for architects and the leaders who choose the stack: default to Postgres, and make every exit justify itself with a number.
cta_body: For every datastore beyond Postgres, can your team name the wall it clears and the number? The ones that can't are consolidation candidates.

slides.caption: One team ran five datastores to serve one product. pgvector, pgmq, TimescaleDB, full-text, JSONB: it all collapses into one Postgres engine.
Five backup stories and five things to patch and get paged about become one backup, one failover, one on-call. The cloud bill drops with them.
Postgres isn't always the answer. Four breakpoints justify a specialist, each named with a number, not a vibe.
Default to Postgres. Hit a wall? Name it with a number. If you can't name the number, you don't have the wall yet.
